resources.string: License_Project_2
• A tangent touches a circle at one point. \n • Circles may touch or intersect. \n • Concentric circles share the same center.
• If R>d, line cuts circle; if R=d, it’s tangent. \n • A tangent is perpendicular to the radius. \n • Tangent segments from one point are equal.
• Tangent parallel to a line found via perpendicular from center. \n • Common tangent between circles uses an auxiliary circle. \n • Both cases show geometric construction steps.
!!! Solve the Quiz !!!
• An arc is part of a circle. \n • A central angle has its vertex at the center. \n • An inscribed angle has its vertex on the circle.
• Arc measure equals its central angle. \n • Inscribed angle equals half the central one. \n • Angles on the same arc are equal; semicircle gives 90°.
• Theorem proof covers three diameter cases. \n • Shows inscribed angle equals half the central one. \n • Demonstrates all geometric configurations.
!!! Solve the Quiz !!!
!!! EMPTY !!!
• The diameter is the longest chord. \n • Equal chords create equal arcs. \n • Diameter perpendicular to a chord bisects it.
• Perpendicular diameter proven using congruent triangles. \n • Parallel chord shown longer than non-parallel one. \n • Geometry demonstrates chord relationships.
!!! Solve the Quiz !!!
• An inscribed polygon has all vertices on a circle. \n • A circumscribed polygon has all sides tangent to a circle. \n • Circles define these polygon relationships.

• Every triangle has one inscribed and one circumscribed circle. \n • For a quadrilateral, opposite angles sum 180° for a circumscribed circle. \n • Equal opposite side sums allow an inscribed circle.
• Opposite angles summing to 180° prove cyclic quadrilateral. \n • Equal tangent lengths prove circumscribed quadrilateral. \n • Demonstrates circle-polygon conditions.
!!! Solve the Quiz !!!
• The centroid is where medians meet. \n • The orthocenter is where altitudes intersect. \n • Both are special triangle points.
• Medians meet at a point two-thirds from each vertex. \n • Altitudes always meet at one point. \n • Defines triangle centers and relations.
• Construct triangle using median lengths. \n • Prove centroid divides median in a 2:1 ratio. \n • Uses parallelogram and midline reasoning.
!!! Solve the Quiz !!!
